excerpt: Imam Jaʿfar al-Ṣādiq stated that faith encompasses testimony (shahādah) in the formula, confirmation of the Prophets and Messengers, affirmation of the Pure Imams, obedience to the Imam, and adherence to Allah's commands whilst abstaining from His prohibitions.
source_work: Rahat al-ʿAql / al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama
source_author: Kirmani, Abū Yaʿqūb al-Sijistānī, or al-Muʾayyad al-Shīrāzī
source_location_hint: Ismaili theological texts on definition of faith and pillars

excerpt: Al-ʿAmir al-Muʾminīn (the Commander of the Faithful, ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib) expressed in Nahj al-Balaghah that faith is conviction of the heart, practice through the limbs, and confession by the tongue.
source_author: al-Sharīf al-Rāḍī (compiler); ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib (originating speaker)
source_location_hint: Nahj al-Balagha; fundamental text on Shiite theology

excerpt: If faith were uniform—without deficiency or excess—then no believer would surpass another in virtue; yet believers enter Paradise by virtue of perfect faith, and gain superiority in God's sight
source_location_hint: Teachings on faith and degrees among believers

excerpt: Faith comprises two branches: outward faith and inward faith
source_location_hint: Doctrine of exoteric and esoteric faith

excerpt: With such inner verification, the inward dimension of faith is established; the deeper the confirmation, the loftier the stations of inner faith
Those whose station constituted the highest veil brought forth Islam; those of inferior station gave birth to faith
source_location_hint: teachings on emanation and cosmic hierarchy

excerpt: The numerical correspondence in the names of Muḥammad and ʿAlī reflects the cosmic order of the Emanators structuring Islamic and spiritual reality
source_location_hint: doctrine of numerical correspondence in divine names

excerpt: The Prophet established the Islamic structure; the Commander of the Faithful established the structure of faith
source_work: Nahj al-Balagha or prophetic hadith tradition
source_author: al-Sharif al-Radi (Nahj) or early Islamic sources
source_location_hint: sayings on the complementary roles of Prophet and Imam
